tasks.dependencies: Fetches the Pricing entity list (stores/zones/segments/vendors) to fan out over
One mapped instance per entity, bound to a dedicated Airflow pool
Runs in parallel with process_unit, watches shared pool capacity
Fan-in of all mapped instances plus the monitor
Evaluates whether queue congestion impacted this or sibling DAGs

incident.description: Queue Congestion incident on the Pricing pipeline (RTL_PRC_QUEUE): ecommerce personalization-model batch-scoring queue congestion.
incident.symptoms: Batch scoring tasks mapped per customer segment (200 segments) queued behind a shared 'ml-inference' pool already saturated by an unrelated recommendation-engine DAG.
incident.root_cause: Two independently-scheduled DAGs (personalization scoring and recommendation-engine refresh) were both assigned to the same 'ml-inference' pool without coordination, and their overlapping schedules regularly exceed the pool's combined capacity needs.
incident.business_impact: Homepage personalization content stales for up to 2 hours during the overlap window, reducing click-through on personalized modules during that period.
incident.resolution: Stagger the two DAGs' schedules to avoid overlap, or split them into separate pools sized independently for each workload's actual needs.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: Two independently-scheduled DAGs (personalization scoring and recommendation-engine refresh) were both assigned to the same 'ml-inference' pool without coordination, and their overlapping schedules regularly exceed the pool's combined capacity needs.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow Pools view attribution by DAG confirms both DAGs' overlapping schedule windows
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog pool.ml_inference.queued_tasks shows two distinct DAGs both contributing to the queue, visible via task-owner tagging
expected_rca.resolution: Stagger the two DAGs' schedules to avoid overlap, or split them into separate pools sized independently for each workload's actual needs.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Add a pool-contention check to the DAG factory validation step that flags newly registered DAGs sharing a pool with existing DAGs whose schedules overlap significantly.
